place: Athens village, New York, New York

facts.text: The Village of Athens sits on the west bank of the Hudson River, just four miles north of the Rip Van Winkle Bridge.
The Town of Athens was established on February 25, 1815, carved from parts of Catskill and Coxsackie.
More than 300 buildings in Athens are listed on national and state historic registers, making it a living museum of American architecture.
Athens’ small, friendly community blends artisans, business people, and families who cherish the village’s historic charm.
The eastern town line follows the Hudson River, marking the border with Columbia County.
Route 9W and the New York State Thruway (I‑87) both cut through the town, linking Athens to the broader region.
